# Chapter 1 System Overview

The IGT ADVANTAGE™M Machine Accounting (MA) system is user friendly and employs the
Microsoft Windows™M operating system. It simplifies the accounting process and provides real-
time network communications of financial information as well as machine and player events
from floor games as they occur.


## 1.1 The Accounting Period

The accounting period is the core of MA. The accounting period separates slot machine play
data into manageable time intervals. After the gaming day is over, the information is collected,
and data is entered for the accounting period. The system collects much of this data; the
auditor is responsible for ensuring its accuracy.

MA can be configured to open the gaming day automatically; however, most properties open
each day manually when they are ready to audit it.


## 1.2 Voucher System

IGT EZ Pay is the typical voucher system used with Machine Accounting. Vouchers are audited
using EZ Pay voucher-audit procedures. Voucher imports into MA are also audited. For more
information on EZ Pay, refer to the EZ Pay guides or online help systems.


## 1.3 Report Features

MA includes many reports to assist in the auditing process. These include reports on the overall
MA system and reports for actual and metered win amounts.


## 1.4 System Components

The following information summarizes the system components integral to the Machine
Accounting system. Review and understand these components, as they are integral to the entire
machine-accounting process.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="13" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 1 System Overview" -->


### Accounting Workstation

Accounting Workstation is the system's central hub and provides a complete record of slot
machine data and activity through its connectivity to the IGT ADVANTAGE Bonusing System.

Features of the Accounting Workstation include:

· A User Tasks pane, which displays the status of complete and incomplete accounting
tasks.

· Maintenance of all aspects of a machine within Machine Accounting.

· Generation of category-specific reports for analysis of casino operations through a
Reports pane.

· Maintenance of floor personnel and their level of access.

· Configuration of Machine Accounting and performance of system administrator
functions.


#### Fills and Jackpot Station (FJP)

Fills and Jackpot Station (FJP) operates with the IGT ADVANTAGE communications network
and posts completed transactions to Machine Accounting. Attendants use the station to
complete hopper fills and handpay jackpots.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/14.1.png)




Configure FJP Server in Configuration Workstation (CWS). This component of the
IGT ADVANTAGE system is not covered in this guide.


##### Attendant Workstation

The Attendant Workstation is typically a touchscreen workstation used for processing the
following information:

· Activating and deactivating attendant cards.

· Activating audits, coin tests, soft drops, hard drops, and combined drop cards.

· Overriding on- and off-shift information or promoting an attendant's job type.

· Issuing and returning attendant equipment at the beginning and end of a shift.

The workstation also includes a card encoder for staff cards and a barcode scanner for issuing
equipment.


##### Drop Utility

Users can configure the IGT ADVANTAGE Drop Utility to work with various interface
hardware components such as coin scales, bill counters and ticket scanners. Located in the
hard- and/or soft-count rooms, the output from the various hardware components is designed
to streamline the accounting process and increase the property's accounting efficiency.


##### Bonusing System

The IGT ADVANTAGE Bonusing System is a complete data-collection and bonusing system
using proprietary technology. The system gathers play information from electronic gaming
machines (EGMs) for use in determining bonus promotions. In addition, the bonusing system
transmits data to the Machine Accounting System.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/14.2.png)




Configure the Bonusing System in CWS. This guide does not cover the Bonusing
System.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="14" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 1 System Overview" -->

These bonusing programs allow a property to promote awards for active players, such as
additional progressive or random jackpots, or awards that are two or three times a standard
jackpot. Casino-wide bonuses are also available. These bonuses use bonusing technology to
control the machine payout of the smaller jackpots and to notify players and floor staff of the
larger jackpots.

The bonusing system includes sounds, meters (displays), flashing lights, and bonus buttons for
a complete multi-sensory promotion.


##### Ticket Redemption Terminal

Use the Ticket Redemption Terminal used to redeem tickets, validate tickets, and balance ticket
batches. In addition, audit individual tickets at the Ticket Redemption Terminal.


##### Radio Pager

Use the Radio Pager interface to set up zoning information for the paging server and alert floor
staff to events. The Pager Trigger monitors the events that trigger a floor page. The Paging
Zone program assigns specific staff positions to floor-paging zones.

## 1.6 The Accounting Period

The primary concept of the Machine Accounting system is the accounting period. An
accounting period generally lasts 24 hours (some properties use longer accounting periods) and
separates slot machine play data into manageable time intervals. After the accounting period is
closed, the system collects and enters play data for the accounting period.

Machine Accounting allows multiple open periods at any given time. The oldest open
accounting period must be closed before closing more recent accounting periods.

The following table outlines a sample of the daily accounting cycle:


<table>
<tr>
<th>Task Number</th>
<th>Task Details</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1</td>
<td>Open Period</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2</td>
<td>Verify Machine Changes (Add/Move/Retire)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3</td>
<td>Adjust Meter Variances, Input Manual Meters</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>4</td>
<td>Reconcile Fills, Jackpots, and Cancel Credits</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>5</td>
<td>Run Meter Reports</td>
</tr>
</table>


<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->

<td>Reconcile Vouchers</td>
</tr>

## 1.7 Machine Data Records

Each machine on a casino floor is treated as an independent business that attracts and
entertains casino guests. Managing these businesses (machines) accurately requires detailed
machine play and accounting records. The IGT Advantage Machine Accounting System collects
and storesthese records


### Casino/Slot Manager

Various reports are generated from the collected information, which allows casino managers to
monitor the performance of each machine and offers information to assist in decision-making
processes for games and machines on the floor.


### State Regulators

State regulators collect income information, monitor machine performance, and evaluate game
integrity using IGT Advantage Machine Accounting reports.


### Slot Technicians

Problems disclosed in the data allow slot technicians to track, troubleshoot, and fix machine
malfunctions.


### Accuracy

Machine Accounting reports are useful only if the entered information is accurate and
consistent. Report data is obtained from a variety of sources, including the following:

· ADVANTAGE Bonusing System-Connects Machine Accounting to the casino
floor. Meters on each machine provide information on various bonuses and machine
problems. Information on hand-paid jackpot slips, progressive slips, and fill slips comes
from the Fills and Jackpots station or from other sources, and is entered by operators.

· Drop System-Provides information on actual currency, vouchers, and coin drop for
each machine. This information can also be entered manually at a Machine Accounting
workstation.

· Machine Records-Machine information is entered into the system at a Machine
Accounting workstation.
